question: When more than 500 tokens must be canceled or moved on a single flow node nested in a multi-instance body, which approach does the Operate documentation recommend?

options.text: Increase the per-operation token limit in Operate's settings, then re-issue the same modification.
Schedule the modification at off-peak hours so Operate processes more tokens per apply.
Cancel the entire process instance and recreate it with execution starting at the target flow node.
Modify individual element instances via the instance history, or use the Orchestration Cluster API with a custom filter.

a.text: The 500-token cap is a technical limit in this version, not a tunable Operate setting.

b.text: Scheduling has no effect on the per-modification token cap.

c.text: Cancelling and recreating destroys instance state and is not the recommended workaround.

d.text: The docs point to single-instance modification (select specific element instances in the instance history) or the Orchestration Cluster API with a custom filter as the recommended way to go beyond 500 tokens.

explanation: Beyond the 500-token-per-modification cap, the docs recommend either single-instance modification by selecting individual element instances in the instance history, or using the Orchestration Cluster API (`modify-process-instance`) with a custom filter for more granular targeting.
